title: Decode Failure and Malformed Packet Detection
app: `Cisco Cyber Vision Splunk Add-On` (Splunkbase 5748) ¬∑ `Nozomi Networks Universal Add-on` (Splunkbase 6905)
spl:
index=nozomi sourcetype="nozomi:alert" type_id="PROTOCOL-DECODE*"
| bin _time span=1h
| stats count as failures by zone, _time
| where failures > 10
| table _time, zone, failures
| sort -failures
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Intrusion_Detection.IDS_Attacks by IDS_Attacks.action, IDS_Attacks.signature, IDS_Attacks.src, IDS_Attacks.dest span=1h | sort - count
description: OT security platforms generate decode failure events when their DPI engine encounters packets it cannot properly parse ‚Äî potentially indicating protocol fuzzing attacks, corrupted communications, or deliberately malformed exploit payloads targeting OT devices. Sustained decode failures from a single source may indicate active exploitation attempts.

detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Cisco Cyber Vision Splunk Add-On` (Splunkbase 5748) ¬∑ `Nozomi Networks Universal Add-on` (Splunkbase 6905).
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype=cisco:cybervision:syslog` ¬∑ Nozomi: `sourcetype=nozomi:alert`.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Both platforms generate events when DPI encounters unparseable packets. Baseline normal decode failure rates per sensor/zone. Alert on sudden spikes exceeding 3x baseline, which may indicate a fuzzing or exploitation attempt. Correlate with IDS alerts from the same time window.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=nozomi sourcetype="nozomi:alert" type_id="PROTOCOL-DECODE*"
| bin _time span=1h
| stats count as failures by zone, _time
| where failures > 10
| table _time, zone, failures
| sort -failures
```

Understanding this SPL

**Decode Failure and Malformed Packet Detection** ‚Äî OT security platforms generate decode failure events when their DPI engine encounters packets it cannot properly parse ‚Äî potentially indicating protocol fuzzing attacks, corrupted communications, or deliberately malformed exploit payloads targeting OT devices. Sustained decode failures from a single source may indicate active exploitation attempts.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=cisco:cybervision:syslog` ¬∑ Nozomi: `sourcetype=nozomi:alert`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Cisco Cyber Vision Splunk Add-On` (Splunkbase 5748) ¬∑ `Nozomi Networks Universal Add-on` (Splunkbase 6905). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: nozomi; **sourcetype**: nozomi:alert. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=nozomi, sourcetype="nozomi:alert". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ Discretizes time or numeric ranges with `bin`/`bucket`.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by zone, _time** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where failures > 10` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ Pipeline stage (see **Decode Failure and Malformed Packet Detection**): table _time, zone, failures
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Intrusion_Detection.IDS_Attacks by IDS_Attacks.action, IDS_Attacks.signature, IDS_Attacks.src, IDS_Attacks.dest span=1h | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Decode Failure and Malformed Packet Detection** ‚Äî OT security platforms generate decode failure events when their DPI engine encounters packets it cannot properly parse ‚Äî potentially indicating protocol fuzzing attacks, corrupted communications, or deliberately malformed exploit payloads targeting OT devices. Sustained decode failures from a single source may indicate active exploitation attempts.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=cisco:cybervision:syslog` ¬∑ Nozomi: `sourcetype=nozomi:alert`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Cisco Cyber Vision Splunk Add-On` (Splunkbase 5748) ¬∑ `Nozomi Networks Universal Add-on` (Splunkbase 6905). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Intrusion_Detection.IDS_Attacks` ‚Äî enable acceleration for that model.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Decode failure timeline per sensor; spike detection; correlation with IDS events; sensor health overlay.
visualization: Decode failure timeline per sensor; spike detection; correlation with IDS events; sensor health overlay.
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern ‚Äî correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
